overview.description: Research group at CTU Prague advancing computer vision and visual recognition
overview.description_long: The Visual Recognition Group (VRG) at Czech Technical University in Prague is a research team dedicated to pushing the boundaries of computer vision. Part of the Center for Machine Perception (CMP) and the Department of Cybernetics at the Faculty of Electrical Engineering, VRG works on object recognition, scene analysis, vision-language applications, 3D reconstruction, image retrieval and matching, video tracking, visual localization, human pose estimation, and species identification. Led by Prof. Jiří Matas (Editor-in-Chief of IJCV), the group publishes extensively at top venues including CVPR, ICCV, and ECCV.
overview.tagline: Pushing the boundaries of computer vision

history.origin_story: Research group at CTU Prague Faculty of Electrical Engineering, affiliated with the Center for Machine Perception. Led by Prof. Jiří Matas, a world-renowned computer vision researcher and Editor-in-Chief of the International Journal of Computer Vision (IJCV).

key_milestones.event: Best poster award at IEEE FG 2024 (Matas & Purkrábek)
8 papers at CVPR 2025 and 5 papers at ICCV 2025
Minister of the Interior Award 2025 for FACIS project
MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowship awarded for RAVIOLI project (Bill Psomas)
Google Research Grant awarded to Giorgos Tolias
GA ČR grant awarded to Giorgos Tolias for Instance-level Visual Recognition and Generation
GA ČR Standard grant awarded to Tomáš Vojíř
Co-organizing Vision and Sports Summer School 2026 in Prague

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Research group at a public university. Produces foundational computer vision research published at top venues (CVPR, ICCV, ECCV). Academic mission is not subject to commercial AGI displacement.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Grant-funded academic research group within Czech Technical University. Not competing in a commercial market; sustained by university funding, national grants (GA ČR), and EU funding (MSCA fellowships).
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Part of CTU Prague, funded through university budget, Czech Science Foundation (GA ČR) grants, EU MSCA fellowships, and Google Research Gifts. Multiple active grants secured as of 2025.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Academic research group with stable institutional backing at CTU Prague, a leading European technical university. Prof. Matas is a world-class researcher (Editor-in-Chief of IJCV) and the group consistently publishes at top-tier venues, ensuring continued funding opportunities.

metadata.notes: VRG is listed as 'company' category in the index but is clearly a research group (category: research-groups on CNAIP). Adapted JSON schema for research group per skill guidelines. Address: Karlovo namesti 13, 121 35 Prague 2, Czech Republic. Contact: matas@fel.cvut.cz
